scope: macOS + Web apps with potential enterprise data leakage risk

app_level_identification: Prefer app-branded SNI/DNS domains (for example, *.doubao* or *.gemini*) as high-precision indicators.
Use exact hostnames and brand tokens before broad provider domains.
Treat file/upload subdomains as stronger data-exfiltration indicators than generic API calls.
For suffix match, implement a label-boundary rule: host == pattern OR host ends with '.' + pattern (avoid naive string-suffix false positives like evilchatgpt.com).
provider_level_identification: Separate AI service providers from broad platform/service domains before scoring.
Use role=ai_service_provider for model/API providers (for example openai.com, anthropic.com, zhipu.ai).
Use role=platform_service for broad domains (for example github.com, qq.com, cloud/CDN/storage domains) that are not app-specific identity.
Treat both as supporting evidence unless combined with app_brand or file-semantics signals.
f1_guidance: High precision rules: exact app domains + brand keyword + file-domain hit.
High recall rules: include provider domains but lower rule score.
Combine DNS, TLS SNI, process/app metadata, and destination ASN for final decision.
Some apps (P2P sync/torrent) have low DNS/TLS signal for the data plane; rely on host artifacts and protocol heuristics when available.

categories.mece_boundary: Agent frameworks, workflow builders, and LLM app orchestration platforms (SaaS/self-host).
Browser products with integrated AI agent capabilities.
System-wide desktop assistants or LLM desktop clients.
Claw-branded AI assistants, OpenClaw derivatives, and closely related claw-family runtimes with local execution, messaging control, or skills/plugin ecosystems.
LLM observability/proxy/tracing tools that can log prompts, responses, and tool outputs.
Persistent cloud file storage and sync products.
Online document/PDF conversion services requiring file upload.
Ephemeral or ad-hoc file transfer services.
Meeting recording, bot joining, and transcription services.
Paste/snippet sharing sites used to publish code and text externally.
Remote desktop/control channels with possible file transfer.
Screen/video recording tools with cloud sharing.
Translation tools (web/app) where users paste or upload sensitive text/documents.
Peer-to-peer sync/torrent clients with uncontrolled file transfer paths.

popularity.notes: Popularity is split into level + region; avoid mixing region into a single enum.

items.title: Prompt/Text Exfiltration
items.description: Users paste confidential text/code into chat/prompt fields; prompts/responses may be stored externally.

items.title: Code Context Exfiltration
items.description: IDE/plugin/agent uploads code context, diffs, repo metadata, or embeddings to external services.

items.title: Unmanaged File Sync
items.description: Persistent sync clients move files to personal/unmanaged cloud drives.

items.title: Ad-hoc File Transfer
items.description: One-off transfer services send files externally via links or direct transfer.

items.title: Public/External Share Links
items.description: Share-link misconfiguration exposes files/docs outside the organization.

items.title: External Doc/Knowledge Collaboration
items.description: Docs/knowledge bases sync to external tenants; guest/public pages leak internal info.

items.title: Meeting Audio Upload
items.description: Audio/video from meetings uploaded for transcription or analysis.

items.title: Transcript/Summary Storage
items.description: Transcripts, summaries, and meeting artifacts retained externally.

items.title: Screen Recording Upload
items.description: Screen recordings or videos captured and shared externally.

items.title: Translation Upload
items.description: Text/documents uploaded to translation services; common accidental leak path.

items.title: Document Conversion Upload
items.description: PDF/Doc conversion tools require uploading entire files for processing.

items.title: Paste/Snippet Sharing
items.description: Code/text posted to paste sites or gists; high risk of secrets exposure.

items.title: Remote Control Channel
items.description: Remote desktop/control bypasses endpoint controls; often includes file transfer/clipboard.

items.title: Plugin/Skill Supply Chain
items.description: Third-party extensions/skills/tools can execute code and exfiltrate data.

items.title: Credential/API Key Exposure
items.description: API keys, tokens, cookies, or secrets may be stored, proxied, or leaked via prompts.

items.title: LLM Observability/Proxy Logging
items.description: Tracing/proxy tools can log full prompts, tool outputs, and responses unless redacted.

items.title: Unmanaged Tenant Use
items.description: Personal/unmanaged workspaces/tenants used instead of corporate-managed environments.

items.title: Agentic Automation
items.description: Agents can browse, run commands, edit files, and call tools leading to accidental exfiltration.

items.title: Biometric/Identity Media Upload
items.description: Voice/face or identity media uploaded to external services.

items.title: History/Retention
items.description: Conversation history, search history, or captured context retained externally.

changes: Add JSON Schema ($schema) and formal taxonomy.
Remove product_form redundancy; standardize on product_shape.
Normalize popularity into {level, region}.
Add structured weighted fingerprints (network + host artifacts).
Introduce risk taxonomy with severity and legacy mapping.
Add traceability hooks (source_ids, last_reviewed_at).
Add P2P_FILE_SHARING category and P2P/torrent tools.
Add additional cloud storage/file transfer/paste/AI desktop apps (MediaFire, Yandex Disk, Seafile, Snapdrop/PairDrop, file.io, Hastebin, Ollama, GPT4All, Open WebUI).
Upgrade macOS host artifacts to structured objects; add local bundle/team/process identifiers where available.
Backfill normalized risk_profile for apps that lacked mapped risks.
Add traceability (source_ids + last_verified_at) for Slack/Box/Dropbox/OneDrive domain fingerprints.
Add additional public OSINT catalogs (blackmatrix7, loyalsoldier).
Tighten schema enums for hostname roles and file semantics.
Normalize provider hostname patterns from exact to suffix for boundary-safe subdomain recall.
Recalibrate per-app weighted_sum thresholds to signal mix and ensure threshold reachability.
Backfill explicit heuristic provenance for previously unattributed rules and app evidence.
Set verification_status and last_verified_at for hostname/host artifacts to improve auditability.
Tighten JSON Schema additionalProperties controls to reduce silent schema drift.
Add risk_profile.priority_score for finer triage beyond coarse severity buckets.
Add host.macos.host_signal_status to explicitly track host telemetry coverage (verified/heuristic/missing).
Refresh evidence.last_reviewed_at timestamps to 2026-02-11 after quality remediation pass.
Prune unreferenced source records; keep only source IDs used by app evidence/fingerprints.
Fact-corrected Skype entry with official retirement date (2025-05-05) and marked signatures as legacy indicators.
Updated OpenHands network identifiers to current openhands.dev/all-hands.dev branding; removed stale openhands.ai from active matching.
Downgraded usyncapp tracker indicators from verified to heuristic due weak historical-only evidence.
Added authoritative sources for Skype retirement and OpenHands canonical domains.
Added external intelligence source records (official docs, official changelogs, and curated GitHub intelligence feeds) for domain-app verification.
Linked source_ids on key IOC hostname patterns (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, OpenRouter, Gemini, Cherry Studio, Tencent Docs, Tencent Translate, GitHub Copilot) to improve auditability.
Updated GitHub Copilot IOC coverage with newly documented routing hosts: api.business.githubcopilot.com, api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com, and api.individual.githubcopilot.com.
Reduced high-FP short keyword risk by lowering weight/precision for short ASCII keyword tokens and replacing Yi Chat keyword 'yi' with 'yi chat'.
Added verified host process indicators for high-risk CLI apps (Claude Code: claude, Aider: aider) with official source traceability.
Added LobeChat as a popular open-source multi-provider AI app with branded IOC domains and linked intelligence sources.
Expanded supporting intelligence records for host/process verification and GitHub popularity coverage.
Added popular GitHub AI apps missing from prior catalog pass: LibreChat, NextChat, and Langflow.
Added traceable official source records for LibreChat/NextChat/Langflow repositories and Langflow docs.
Linked branded and provider IOC hostname patterns for new app entries with explicit source_ids.
Retried watcha-finder reference lookup; repository not discoverable via current public index in this environment, so fallback enrichment used direct canonical repositories.
Verified first-wave enterprise apps against official sources: AnyDesk, TeamViewer, WeTransfer, Telegram, ChatGPT, Claude, Google Drive, and GitHub Copilot.
Added authoritative source records for official product domains, firewall allowlists, and vendor documentation used in network detection.
Promoted directly supported hostname patterns from heuristic to verified where official sources confirmed branded domains or provider endpoints.
Added newly documented high-signal infrastructure domains net.anydesk.com and wetransfer.net for stronger enterprise monitoring coverage.
Added CLAW_FAMILY_APP taxonomy for claw-branded AI assistant and runtime products that previously blended into broader assistant/framework buckets.
Verified and added QClaw, ZeroClaw, NanoClaw, and PicoClaw from official sources; reclassified OpenClaw into the new claw-family category.
Captured official brand domains and download endpoints for claw-family detection, including Tencent QClaw, zeroclawlabs.ai, nanoclaw.dev, and PicoClaw download hosts.
Imported and enriched the claw-agent landscape CSV into the main catalog with per-row TODO tracking.
Added or updated claw-adjacent desktop, coding, browser, and framework apps with repo/site-derived IOC hostname patterns.
Captured repo-visible macOS host indicators such as directory paths and bundle identifiers where authoritative project materials exposed them.
Manually reviewed claw-family IOC data for OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and ZeroClaw instead of relying on repo-string auto-extraction.
Removed false-positive hostname indicators derived from code symbols, README badges, and unrelated external/social links.
Added typed macOS host path indicators so bundle, binary, config, state, log, and skills paths can be distinguished during detection.
